limitation_source: recurrence_risk_features_mv stores boolean recurrence_flag without associated calendar date; no structured recurrence registry exists in ehr
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: The recurrence flag is derived from a structured boolean field that does not carry a calendar date. Day-level date recovery would require chart abstraction of all 1986 recurrence-positive patients.
safe_methods_sentence: Recurrence events were classified using a structured composite flag (recurrence_risk_features_mv); calendar dates were available for 222/1986 recurrence-positive patients (54 exact + 168 biochemical inflection). The remaining 1764 patients (88.8%) had a confirmed recurrence event but no recoverable calendar date from available data sources.
safe_results_sentence: Recurrence was documented in 1986 of 10871 patients (18.3%). A calendar date for the recurrence event was available in 222 patients (11.2%): 54 with an exact source date and 168 with a biochemical inflection date. Kaplan-Meier analyses incorporating time-to-recurrence were limited to these 222 patients.
safe_limitations_sentence: The absence of a structured recurrence date registry limits time-to-event analyses to a minority of recurrence-positive patients. The primary recurrence endpoint is binary (recurrence yes/no); survival analyses treating recurrence as a timed endpoint carry selection bias risk.

limitation_source: nuclear medicine treatment records and dispensing logs are not present in the clinical_notes_long corpus; structured rai_treatment_episode_v2 captures receipt but not dose for 59% of treated patients
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: RAI dose was documented in only 41% of treated episodes because nuclear medicine dispensing records were not electronically linked to the research database. NLP recovery is capped by the absence of endocrine note dose documentation for most patients.
safe_methods_sentence: Radioactive iodine (RAI) dose (mCi) was available from NLP extraction of endocrine notes and discharge summaries for 761/1857 RAI treatment episodes (41.0%). Nuclear medicine dispensing records were not available in the source corpus; accordingly dose analyses are restricted to the available subset with appropriate missingness notation.
safe_results_sentence: RAI was administered in 862 patients; dose documentation was available for 761 treatment episodes (41.0%; median dose 142 mCi where available). Among the subset with dose data...
safe_limitations_sentence: RAI dose was unrecoverable for 59% of treated patients because nuclear medicine administration records were not included in the research corpus. Published dose norms were used for context but not imputed.

limitation_source: pth values were extracted via nlp from note free text; nlp extraction does not yield structured laboratory accession dates; only clinical_notes_long note_date is available
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: PTH collection date cannot be recovered computationally: the values were parsed from free-text note bodies where no machine-readable accession/collection date was embedded. Achieving temporal precision requires institutional laboratory data extract.
safe_methods_sentence: Postoperative parathyroid hormone (PTH) values were extracted from 797 patients via natural language processing of clinical notes (primarily endocrine and discharge notes). A laboratory collection date was available for 139 patients (17.4%) via structured sources; the remaining values carry only note-level date resolution (±14 days). PTH was used for descriptive characterisation of hypocalcaemia and hypoparathyroidism; it was not used as a time-to-event endpoint.
safe_results_sentence: PTH was available in 797 patients (7.3% of cohort); temporal precision was limited (collection date available in 17.4% of PTH-positive patients). Postoperative nadir PTH was below 15 pg/mL in 11 patients.
safe_limitations_sentence: Postoperative PTH values were extracted from clinical notes and lack laboratory-grade temporal precision for the majority of patients. Analyses requiring PTH as a timed endpoint were not performed.

limitation_source: calcium values were extracted via nlp from note free text; same limitation as pth; institutional calcium lab table not available
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Calcium collection dates cannot be computed from available sources. The electronic institutional laboratory extract covering calcium was not incorporated.
safe_methods_sentence: Total serum calcium was available from NLP extraction for 598 patients (5.5% of cohort). Laboratory collection dates were confirmed for 69 patients (11.5%); the remainder carry note-date precision only. Calcium was used solely for descriptive characterisation of biochemical hypocalcaemia and was not used as a timed endpoint.
safe_results_sentence: Postoperative calcium was documented in 598 patients; collection date was confirmable for 11.5%. Hypocalcaemia (Ca <8.0 mg/dL) was identified in 5 patients.
safe_limitations_sentence: Calcium temporal coverage is insufficient for postoperative-day–level analyses. An institutional laboratory data feed would be required to support these analyses.

limitation_source: tsh and free_t4 measurements are not present as a derived table in the research database; lab results are not captured in any of the 8 raw excel source files
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: TSH/free_T4 data does not exist in any exploited source. Additional computation on existing tables cannot create this data; institutional EHR lab extraction would be required.
safe_methods_sentence: Thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH) and thyroid hormone levels were not available in the research data sources. These values were accordingly not included in primary or sensitivity analyses.
safe_results_sentence: TSH and thyroid hormone measurements were not available in the research database and were not reported.
safe_limitations_sentence: Thyroid function tests (TSH free T4 free T3) are absent from the available data sources. Their incorporation would require a structured institutional laboratory data extract.

limitation_source: clinical_notes_long contains 8 note types (op_note h_p endocrine_note ed_note history_summary dc_sum other_notes other_history); nuclear medicine consultation notes and scan reports are not in this corpus
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Zero nuclear medicine notes are present in the corpus. The note extraction pipeline cannot parse data that was never ingested. Nuclear medicine reporting systems were not linked to the research data extract.
safe_methods_sentence: Nuclear medicine reports including I-131 dosimetry and diagnostic whole-body scan findings were not available in the clinical note corpus. RAI receipt was inferred from structured and NLP sources within endocrine and discharge notes.
safe_results_sentence: Nuclear medicine scan reports were absent from the clinical notes corpus. RAI dose and diagnostic scan results could not be extracted from dedicated nuclear medicine documentation.
safe_limitations_sentence: The absence of nuclear medicine reports from the source corpus is a primary structural limitation for RAI characterisation. RAI dose and scan findings were estimated from endocrine and discharge notes only.

limitation_source: path_synoptics synoptic template marks vascular invasion as 'x' (present) without vessel count or focal/extensive sub-field; 4652/5570 (83.5%) positive cases carry only "present_ungraded"
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: The present_ungraded category reflects a synoptic template limitation: the source pathology reporting form did not require vessel count at time of sign-out. Computational re-grading requires individual pathology report free-text review by a pathologist for 4652 cases.
safe_methods_sentence: Vascular invasion was recorded in 5570 patients (51.3% of cohort). WHO 2022 grading (focal <4 vessels; extensive ≥4 vessels) was determinable in 819 patients (14.7% of vascular-invasion-positive cases): 463 focal and 356 extensive. The remaining 4652 positive cases (83.5%) were recorded as present but ungraded because the source synoptic pathology template did not capture vessel count.
safe_results_sentence: Vascular invasion was present in 5570 patients (51.3%); WHO 2022 sub-grade was available in 819 vascular-positive patients (14.7%): focal in 463 and extensive in 356. The majority (83.5%) carried a present-ungraded designation consistent with the synoptic template used during the study period.
safe_limitations_sentence: WHO 2022 vascular invasion grading was not uniformly ascertainable because the institutional synoptic pathology template did not mandate vessel count documentation. Graded analyses were restricted to the 14.7% with quantified vessel counts.

limitation_source: the entity type esophageal_involvement is not in the note_entities_procedures nlp vocabulary; operative episode v2 reports 0 cases because no entity was ever extracted
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Adding the esophageal_involvement entity to the NLP vocabulary and re-running extraction would yield estimates from operative notes. However this would require vocabulary expansion and re-extraction rather than recomputation from existing fields. Currently 0 values are NOT confirmed negatives — they are unparsed.
safe_methods_sentence: Esophageal involvement during thyroidectomy was not reliably captured by the NLP extraction pipeline, as this entity was absent from the procedure entity vocabulary. The field is not interpretable as 0% prevalence; analyses requiring esophageal involvement were not performed.
safe_results_sentence: Esophageal involvement could not be ascertained from the available extraction pipeline. This field was excluded from primary and sensitivity analyses.
safe_limitations_sentence: The operative NLP extraction pipeline did not include esophageal involvement in its entity vocabulary during the study period. The zero count reflects an unextracted field not a confirmed clinical finding.

limitation_source: berry_ligament entity not in note_entities_procedures vocabulary; same as esophageal: zero values are NOT confirmed negatives
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Same as SL-08: entity vocabulary gap. Cannot be solved by recomputation from existing tables.
safe_methods_sentence: Berry ligament management was not ascertainable from the available extraction pipeline; this entity was absent from the operative procedure vocabulary.
safe_results_sentence: Berry ligament data was not available from NLP extraction and was not reported.
safe_limitations_sentence: Operative NLP entity vocabulary did not include berry ligament. This field was not analysed.

limitation_source: frozen_section_flag entity not in note_entities_procedures vocabulary
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Same as SL-08. Cannot be solved by recomputation.
safe_methods_sentence: Intraoperative frozen section use was not captured by the operative NLP pipeline (entity vocabulary absent). This field was not reported.
safe_results_sentence: Frozen section data was not ascertained from available sources and was not reported.
safe_limitations_sentence: Intraoperative frozen section utilisation was not extractable from existing NLP vocabulary.

limitation_source: ebl_ml entity not in note_entities_procedures vocabulary; structured operative_details.bmi coverage is 1.3% (122/9368 rows) and ebl is not in that table
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Same as SL-08. Cannot be solved by recomputation.
safe_methods_sentence: Estimated blood loss (EBL) was not systematically captured by the operative NLP extraction pipeline.
safe_results_sentence: EBL data was not available from the extraction pipeline and was not reported.
safe_limitations_sentence: Estimated blood loss was not captured in operative NLP vocabulary. Structured EBL fields were absent from available source tables; accordingly EBL was not reported.

limitation_source: clinical_notes_long contains 11037 notes for 5641 patients (51.9%); the remaining 48.1% have structured pathology and lab data but no clinical notes in the research database extract
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Note corpus coverage cannot be increased computationally. The 48.1% without notes were not included in the note extraction pipeline.
safe_methods_sentence: Clinical notes (operative notes, H&P, endocrine notes, discharge summaries, and related types) were available for 5641 of 10871 patients (51.9%). NLP-derived variables (molecular marker context, operative findings, complication entity extraction) were ascertained from this subset. Structured pathology and laboratory variables were available for the full cohort independent of note availability.
safe_results_sentence: Clinical notes were available for 5641 patients (51.9% of cohort). NLP-derived variables were accordingly restricted to this subset; structured variables were available cohort-wide.
safe_limitations_sentence: Approximately half of the surgical cohort had no clinical notes in the research extract. NLP-derived variables may under-represent the full cohort; structured pathology variables are not subject to this limitation.

limitation_source: ve1 ihc pathology addendum reports are not in clinical_notes_long; only 2 ihc braf results were identifiable across all note types
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: IHC VE1 pathology addendum system is not linked to the clinical_notes_long corpus. Re-running NLP on existing notes will yield at most the same 2 results observed.
safe_methods_sentence: IHC BRAF (VE1 antibody) documentation was available in only 2 patients due to the absence of pathology addendum reports from the clinical notes corpus. BRAF positivity was determined primarily from molecular testing platform data (NGS n=361; NLP-confirmed entity n=173; total n=546). IHC-based BRAF status was not used as a primary analytic variable.
safe_results_sentence: IHC-confirmed BRAF (VE1) was available in 2 patients and was not used for primary analysis. BRAF status was determined from molecular sequencing platforms.
safe_limitations_sentence: IHC BRAF (VE1) data were unavailable for the vast majority of patients because pathology addendum reports were not included in the clinical notes extract.

limitation_source: path_tumor_size_cm extracted from path_synoptics.tumor_1_size_greatest_dimension_cm; 62% missing reflects incomplete synoptic documentation in earlier cases and benign procedures without formal tumor measurement
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Tumor size missingness reflects incomplete source documentation; it cannot be computed from other available fields. MICE imputation was applied for analytic samples requiring this variable.
safe_methods_sentence: Pathological tumor size (greatest dimension in cm) was available for 4130 of 10871 patients (38.0%) from synoptic pathology reports. For analyses requiring tumor size as a covariate multiple imputation by chained equations (MICE m=20) was used with cancer diagnostic category and patient demographics as auxiliary variables.
safe_results_sentence: Tumor size was available in 4130 patients (38.0%); median 1.6 cm [IQR]. For multivariable models MICE imputation was applied across 20 imputed datasets.
safe_limitations_sentence: Pathological tumor size was missing in 62.0% of patients primarily reflecting benign procedures and early cases with incomplete synoptic documentation. All analyses incorporating tumor size were repeated using MICE-imputed values.

limitation_source: ln_pos count from synoptic pathology; missing in 66.9% reflecting benign procedures and partial lymph node documentation; MICE imputation applied
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Same missingness source as SL-14. MICE handles this for analytic samples.
safe_methods_sentence: Positive lymph node count was available from synoptic pathology for 3603 patients (33.1%). For multivariable analyses MICE imputation (m=20) was applied using available staging and diagnostic covariates.
safe_results_sentence: Positive lymph nodes were documented in 3603 patients (33.1%); [value] patients were LN-positive.
safe_limitations_sentence: Lymph node count was missing in 66.9% of patients primarily in benign procedures and early cases. MICE imputation was used for multivariable analyses requiring this variable.

limitation_source: ajcc8 requires histology_type + tumor_size + ln_status + distant_mets; calculable only when all inputs non-null; 62.4% not calculable because benign procedures and missing tumor_size/ln_pos prevent calculation
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: AJCC8 staging cannot be computed without the required clinical inputs. MICE imputation resolves the numeric missingness; histology requirement limits staging to malignant cases (4136 cancer-eligible).
safe_methods_sentence: AJCC 8th edition staging was calculable in 4083 of 10871 patients (37.6%) and in 3996 of 4136 cancer-eligible patients (96.6%). Calculation required: histological diagnosis of thyroid malignancy, pathological tumour size, lymph node status, and absence of distant metastases. Benign procedures (n=6735) were not staged.
safe_results_sentence: AJCC 8th edition staging was calculable in 4083 patients overall (37.6%) and in 96.6% of cancer-eligible patients (n=4136).
safe_limitations_sentence: AJCC 8th edition staging was limited to patients with a confirmed malignant histological diagnosis and available tumour size and nodal status data. Benign procedures comprising 62% of the surgical cohort were not staged.

limitation_source: ata 2015 initial risk requires histology + ete_grade + vascular_invasion + ln_count + molecular markers; lower calculability than ajcc8 because ete_grade and vascular grading are missing for more patients
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: ATA calculability is limited by input completeness. Once required staging variables are determined from pathology records the score is computed deterministically.
safe_methods_sentence: ATA 2015 initial risk stratification was calculable in 3144 patients (28.9% of cohort; 76.0% of cancer-eligible patients). Required inputs were: histological diagnosis, ETE grade, vascular invasion status, lymph node characteristics, and age at surgery.
safe_results_sentence: ATA 2015 initial risk was calculable in 3144 patients; high risk n=XXX (XX.X%) intermediate risk n=XXX (XX.X%) low risk n=XXX (XX.X%).
safe_limitations_sentence: ATA initial risk was calculable in 76.0% of cancer-eligible patients; remaining patients lacked sufficient pathological detail for stratification.

limitation_source: imaging structured us data from COMPLETE_MULTI_SHEET_ULTRASOUND_REPORTS.xlsx covers 4074 patients; serial_imaging_us.dominant_nodule_size_on_us is entirely null; US reports from outside institutions not available
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: TIRADS coverage is bounded by which patients underwent ultrasound at the study institution and had structured reports in the ingested Excel files. Cannot be increased computationally from current sources.
safe_methods_sentence: Preoperative ultrasound data were available from structured radiology spreadsheets (COMPLETE_MULTI_SHEET_ULTRASOUND_REPORTS.xlsx) for 3474 patients (31.9%). ACR TI-RADS scores were available directly or recalculated from component criteria (composition echogenicity shape margins calcifications). Patients lacking preoperative ultrasound data from this source were excluded from imaging-based analyses; this exclusion may be non-random as external or community ultrasound reports were not captured.
safe_results_sentence: Preoperative TIRADS was available in 3474 patients (31.9%); the majority were TR4 (44.0%) and TR5 (24.1%) consistent with a surgical cohort enriched for high-risk nodules.
safe_limitations_sentence: Preoperative TI-RADS scores were available for only 31.9% of the cohort because ultrasound reports were captured only from the institutional structured reporting system. Patients imaged at outside institutions contributed no imaging data.

limitation_source: path_synoptics records ene as x/present (2389 present_ungraded) without extent; extent grading (focal/microscopic/macroscopic) requires free-text path report parsing; only 169 extensive/focal from multi-source extraction
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: Computational re-grading from synoptic 'x' data is impossible without free-text pathology report parsing. Extent grading was achieved for multi-source patients only.
safe_methods_sentence: Extranodal extension (ENE) was identified in 2558 patients by composite source (structured synoptic extranodal_extension field and multi-source NLP). ENE extent (focal/extensive) was gradable in 178 patients (7.0% of ENE-positive cases) from free-text pathology and operative note review; 2389 cases (83.5%) carried a present-ungraded designation from the synoptic checkbox.
safe_results_sentence: ENE was documented in 2558 patients; extent grading was available in 178 (focal 169; extensive 4; other 5). The majority of ENE cases were present-ungraded.
safe_limitations_sentence: ENE extent grading was limited by the synoptic pathology template which records ENE presence as a binary field. Graduated ENE extent required free-text pathology report parsing and was achievable for only 7.0% of ENE-positive cases.

limitation_source: 31 remain ras_unspecified because molecular_testing free text and genetics raw columns do not specify subtype; thyroseq enrichment resolved 1 additional case; 31 are genuinely unresolvable from available source text
why_computation_cannot_solve_it: 31 RAS_unspecified cases cannot be resolved without requesting original molecular test reports from the testing laboratory.
safe_methods_sentence: RAS mutations were identified in 337 patients (3.1% of cohort). Subtype resolution was successful in 317/348 RAS-subtype-queried patients (91.1%): NRAS n=196 HRAS n=114 KRAS n=59. Thirty-one patients remained RAS_unspecified because subtype information was absent from all available data sources.
safe_results_sentence: RAS was mutated in 337 patients (NRAS 196; HRAS 114; KRAS 59; unspecified 31). [additional results].
safe_limitations_sentence: Thirty-one RAS-positive patients had unspecified RAS subtype because sequencing subtype detail was absent from available molecular records. These patients were classified as RAS-positive in analyses not requiring subtype.
